distill_output_instruction: Write all distilled replacements in English. Preserve technical terms (URLs, file paths, commands, version numbers, migration IDs) verbatim — do not translate them.

triage.review_instruction: Review {table_path}, then run `/mp:memory-apply {table_path}` to execute it. The apply step creates a full backup before touching anything.

apply.next_step_suggestion: Run `/mp:memory-compress --dir {dir}` to compress the surviving prose with caveman (if installed). MemPenny removes what shouldn't be there; caveman shrinks what's left.

compress.nothing_to_do: No files match the scope — nothing to compress.
compress.backup_note: Caveman created `FILE.original.md` for every compressed file. Keep them for ~2 weeks; delete only after nothing feels wrong.
compress.rollback_comment_all: Rollback every compressed file in this dir

distill.action_prompt: Apply, skip, or edit the proposal?
distill.already_tight_message: This file is already tight (≤3 sentences, already forward-looking). No distillation needed.
distill.applied_message: Distilled body written. Final size: {size} bytes.
distill.cannot_compress_message: Cannot fit all load-bearing content into 3 sentences without loss. Refusing to lie with a bad distillation.

clean.first_run_intro: MemPenny stores backups before touching your memory files. Where should backups go?
clean.first_run_prompt: Press Enter to accept the default, or type an absolute path
clean.config_saved: Config saved to {path}. Future /mp:clean runs will use this backup folder automatically.
clean.auto_apply_note: Applying changes now. Backup will be created at {path}.
clean.backup_pruning_hint: Backups accumulate over time. Run `ls {backup_root}` periodically and delete old backups you no longer need.

restore.no_backups_found: No backups found in {path}. Nothing to restore.
restore.no_config: No config yet — run /mp:clean once first so MemPenny knows where your backups live.
restore.pick_prompt: Which backup to restore? (number, or name, or 'latest')
restore.confirm_safety: Current memory dir will be moved to {safety} before restore, in case you change your mind.
restore.safety_retention_hint: Safety snapshots accumulate over time. Run `ls -d {memory_dir}.pre-restore-*` periodically to see them; delete ones older than ~2 weeks if nothing feels wrong.

errors.memory_dir_not_found: Could not locate the auto-memory directory. Please supply the absolute path.
errors.table_not_found: Triage table not found at {path}. Run `/mp:memory-triage` first, then pass the printed table path as the first argument to `/mp:memory-apply`.
errors.backup_failed: Backup creation failed. STOPPING. No files were modified.
errors.locale_missing: Locale '{lang}' not found. Falling back to English. Submit a translation PR at https://github.com/marcelopaniza/mempenny/tree/main/locales
errors.caveman_not_installed_prose: Caveman is not installed. MemPenny removes what shouldn't be there; caveman compresses what's left. To install, run the commands printed below, then re-run `/mp:memory-compress --dir {dir}`.
errors.backup_folder_invalid: Backup folder path must be absolute and writable: {path}
